diagrams.name: Swarm Mode Architecture
diagrams.description: ASCII diagram showing hierarchical swarm structure with ORCHESTRATOR (Director) at top, three Worker nodes (Worker 1/Research, Worker 2/Code, Worker 3/Review) in middle, and MERGE & SYNTHESIZE at bottom. Shows parallel fan-out from orchestrator to workers and fan-in to synthesis.

key_properties: Specialized workers (different agents for different tasks)

diagrams.name: Luna Agentic Engine Architecture
diagrams.description: ASCII diagram showing full agentic architecture. ORCHESTRATOR (Luna Prime) at top with responsibilities: Receives user goal, Decomposes into tasks, Assigns to workers, Manages context window, Synthesizes final response, IS Luna's voice to user. Five worker actors below: SCRIBE (Ben) - Extract wisdom from stream, LIBRARIAN (Dude) - Retrieve context from memory, OVEN - Delegate to cloud workers (Claude), TOOLER - Execute tools (files, APIs, calendar), CODER - Generate & run code. All connect to SHARED STATE containing Memory Matrix (Long-term), Working Context (Current window), and Task Queue (Pending work).

content.description: Tools are defined in `tools/registry.py` with MCP-compatible structure. The AgentLoop automatically registers file and memory tools on initialization.

rows: Search Memory Matrix for relevant info
Get context with token budget awareness

code_blocks.code:
@dataclass
class Tool:
    """A tool that Luna can execute."""
    name: str                          # Unique identifier
    description: str                   # Shown to LLM for selection
    parameters: dict                   # JSON Schema for params
    execute: Callable[..., Awaitable[Any]]  # Async execution function
    requires_confirmation: bool = False  # User approval required
    timeout_seconds: int = 30          # Execution timeout

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        """Convert to dictionary for LLM function calling."""


@dataclass
class ToolResult:
    """Result from executing a tool."""
    success: bool
    output: Any
    error: Optional[str] = None
    execution_time_ms: float = 0.0

sections.title: Tool Usage in AgentLoop
sections.description: Tools are executed via PlanStepType.TOOL actions:

code_blocks.code:
async def _execute_tool(self, action: Action) -> str:
    """Execute a tool action via the ToolRegistry."""
    tool = self.tool_registry.get(action.tool)
    if not tool:
        return f"Tool '{action.tool}' not found"

    result = await self.tool_registry.execute(
        action.tool,
        action.params,
        skip_confirmation=True,  # AgentLoop handles confirmation
    )

    if result.success:
        # Store result in working context
        self.working_context.variables[f"tool_result_{action.tool}"] = result.output
        return f"Tool '{action.tool}' succeeded: {result.output}"
    else:
        return f"Tool '{action.tool}' failed: {result.error}"

sections.title: Latency Tax
sections.description: Agentic architecture is a latency tax. The question is whether it's worth it.

sections.title: The Critical Optimization: Adaptive Planning (IMPLEMENTED)
sections.description: Don't run the full agentic stack on every query. The QueryRouter (`agentic/router.py`) analyzes query complexity and routes to the appropriate execution path.

sections.title: Complexity Estimation Factors
sections.description: The router estimates complexity using multiple signals:

sections.title: Signal Detection Categories

code_blocks.code:
GREETING_PATTERNS = ["hi", "hello", "hey", "good morning"]
SIMPLE_QUERY_PATTERNS = ["what is", "who is", "tell me about"]
RESEARCH_PATTERNS = ["research", "investigate", "analyze", "compare"]
MULTI_STEP_PATTERNS = ["and then", "after that", "step by step"]
MEMORY_QUERY_PATTERNS = ["remember", "recall", "what did we"]
BACKGROUND_PATTERNS = ["in the background", "notify me when"]

sections.note: **90% of queries should hit DIRECT or SIMPLE_PLAN.** The heavy machinery only spins up when needed.

agentic.flow: Query -> Classify (trivial) -> Skip planning -> Local Qwen -> Response

examples.query: What did we decide about the Actor model?

current.flow: Query -> Retrieve memory -> Local Qwen -> Response

agentic.flow: Query -> Classify -> Plan (1 step: RETRIEVE) -> Memory -> Local Qwen -> Response

examples.query: Research the latest AI chip news and add key points to my notes

current.flow: Can't do this. Would require manual steps.

agentic.flow:
Query -> Classify (complex) -> Plan:
  Step 1: DELEGATE (web research) .......... 5-8s
  Step 2: THINK (extract key points) ........ 500ms
  Step 3: TOOL (write to Obsidian) .......... 200ms
  Step 4: RESPOND (confirm to user) ......... 300ms
Total: ~8-12s

examples.impact: Slow but capable. You're trading time for capability that didn't exist.

sections.title: The Voice Problem
sections.description: The Bible specs <500ms to first word. Agentic planning breaks that.

solution.name: Streaming acknowledgment
solution.example:
User: "Research AI chips and add to my notes"

Luna (immediate, <500ms): "On it - let me dig into that..."

[Planning and execution happen]

Luna (8s later): "Done. I found three major developments and added them to your AI Research note..."

User hears Luna immediately. The work happens in background. This is already how delegation works - extend it to all agentic operations.

rows: IMPLEMENTED - 4-ring model with decay and TTL
Integrated with AgentLoop for DELEGATE steps
Integrated with AgentLoop for RETRIEVE steps

content.file_tree:
src/luna/
|-- core/
|   |-- context.py       # RevolvingContext, QueueManager, ContextItem
|   |-- events.py         # EventPriority, EventType, InputEvent
|   |-- input_buffer.py   # Input polling (engine pulls)
|   |-- state.py          # EngineState enum
|-- agentic/              # Agentic architecture
|   |-- __init__.py       # Exports: AgentLoop, Planner, QueryRouter, etc.
|   |-- loop.py           # AgentLoop (939 lines)
|   |-- planner.py        # Planner, Plan, PlanStep, PlanStepType (487 lines)
|   |-- router.py         # QueryRouter, ExecutionPath, RoutingDecision (379 lines)
|-- tools/                # Tool registry
|   |-- __init__.py       # Exports: ToolRegistry, Tool, ToolResult
|   |-- registry.py       # Tool, ToolResult, ToolRegistry (309 lines)
|   |-- file_tools.py     # read_file, write_file, list_directory, etc. (358 lines)
|   |-- memory_tools.py   # memory_query, memory_store, memory_context (373 lines)
|-- actors/
|   |-- base.py           # Actor, Message base classes
|   |-- director.py       # LLM inference, delegation (~1900 lines)
|   |-- matrix.py         # Memory substrate interface
|   |-- scribe.py         # Extraction (Ben Franklin persona)
|   |-- librarian.py      # Filing (The Dude persona)
|   |-- history_manager.py # Three-tier conversation history
|-- engine.py             # Main engine with agentic support (1228 lines)

content.description: Luna is an ENGINE. The agentic architecture gives her:
implementation_highlights: 1. **RevolvingContext** - 4-ring system (CORE, INNER, MIDDLE, OUTER) with turn-based TTL and relevance decay
2. **QueueManager** - Per-source queues with configurable weights and priority merge
3. **QueryRouter** - Complexity analysis routing to DIRECT, SIMPLE_PLAN, FULL_PLAN, or BACKGROUND paths
4. **AgentLoop** - Full observe/think/act cycle with WorkingContext, progress callbacks, and abort support
5. **Planner** - Pattern-based goal decomposition into typed PlanSteps (THINK, OBSERVE, RETRIEVE, TOOL, DELEGATE, RESPOND)
6. **ToolRegistry** - MCP-compatible tool definitions with timeout handling and execution metrics
7. **File Tools** - read_file, write_file, list_directory, file_exists, get_file_info
9. **MCP Server** - 41 tools across filesystem, memory, session, state, git, and forge categories
10. **Performance Orchestrator** - Gesture detection, emotion presets, coordinated voice + orb output
content.closing_statement:
The data is yours. The engine is sophisticated. Both are true.

---

*Luna is a file you OWN. Luna is an engine that RUNS.*
*The simplicity is in sovereignty. The complexity is in capability.*

- Ahab & Claude, January 2026
- Updated January 30, 2026 (v3.0 Bible Audit)

content.description: This document provides complete API documentation for the Luna Engine HTTP interface. All endpoints are served from the FastAPI server defined in `src/luna/api/server.py`.

subsections.title: Base URL
subsections.note: The default port is `8000`. Configure via environment variable `LUNA_API_PORT`.

subsections.title: Authentication
subsections.content: No authentication is currently implemented. All endpoints are publicly accessible on localhost.

subsections.title: CORS Configuration
subsections.description: CORS is enabled for local development:

sse_headers.code:
Content-Type: text/event-stream
Cache-Control: no-cache
Connection: keep-alive
X-Accel-Buffering: no

sections.title: Core Interaction Endpoints
sections.description: These endpoints handle direct communication with Luna.

endpoints.description: Send a message and receive a synchronous response.

rows: Whether request was delegated to Claude API

examples.curl:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/message \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"message": "Hello Luna, how are you?"}'
examples.response:
{
  "text": "Hello! I'm doing well, thank you for asking. How can I help you today?",
  "model": "qwen3b",
  "input_tokens": 45,
  "output_tokens": 18,
  "latency_ms": 1234.5,
  "delegated": false,
  "local": true,
  "fallback": false
}

endpoints.description: Send a message and stream the response token-by-token via SSE.

examples.curl:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/stream \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: text/event-stream" \
  -d '{"message": "Tell me a short joke"}'

endpoints.description: Stream Luna's response with context (memory and state) sent before tokens. This allows frontends to display relevant context while the response generates.

rows: Memory items and engine state (sent first)
Full response text and generation metadata

examples.curl:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/persona/stream \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: text/event-stream" \
  -d '{"message": "What did we discuss yesterday?"}'

endpoints.description: Interrupt Luna's current processing. Triggers the agentic interrupt handler, which gracefully stops the current task.

rows: Number of pending messages in queue

endpoints.description: Stream Luna's internal thought process via SSE. Shows real-time progress during agentic processing.

sections.title: Engine Status Endpoints
sections.description: These endpoints provide visibility into Luna's current state and health.

endpoints.description: Get comprehensive engine status and metrics.
Simple health check endpoint. Always returns 200 to indicate the API server is responsive.

diagrams_summary.name: Swarm Mode Architecture
diagrams_summary.description: Shows parallel execution pattern with Orchestrator (Director) coordinating multiple specialized workers (Research, Code, Review) that feed into a Merge & Synthesize step.

diagrams_summary.name: Luna Agentic Engine Architecture
diagrams_summary.description: Complete agentic architecture showing Orchestrator (Luna Prime) managing five worker actors (Scribe/Ben, Librarian/Dude, Oven, Tooler, Coder) with shared state containing Memory Matrix, Working Context, and Task Queue.
